items.prompt: How many bits does one BCD digit use?
items.explain: Each decimal digit is a 4-bit nibble.
Valid digits are 0–9; A–F are invalid.

items.prompt: Decimal 42 as packed BCD (2 digits) is…
items.explain: Nibbles 4 and 2 pack as 0x42; binary 42 is 0x2A.

items.prompt: Nibble 0xA is a valid BCD digit.
items.explain: 0xA–0xF are not decimal digits in BCD.
